response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web/backend experience, but the role specifically requires deep, current C++ expertise alongside industrial automation, secure development under IEC 62443, and protocols like MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), with subsequent career focus on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. None of the must-have areas (industrial cybersecurity SDL, message brokers in C++, MQTT/IoT, multi-threaded networked C++ systems) are demonstrated.
strengths: Strong overall engineering tenure (~13+ years) with senior roles at major tech companies
Solid testing, TDD, and software craftsmanship discipline relevant to SDL practices
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang microservices and prior C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: No recent or substantive C++/STL experience — only ~6 months over a decade ago
No demonstrated industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, or OT-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) background
Career trajectory is web/cloud full-stack, misaligned with on-prem C++ platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / OT-protocol experience
Senior engineering breadth and craftsmanship discipline
